heading: Sixto geometry derivatives now include pixel-frame CCH primitives and a structured H/N/K stage graph
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto-ramos, geometry-derivatives, vectorization, stage-graph]
body: Built `build_sixto_geometry_derivatives.py` and generated two derivative artifacts from the local Arto source images. `cch_archm1b_vectorization.json` now records exact pixel-frame primitives for the CCH overview: left red primary circle `(752.5, 994.0, r=268.54)`, left red lower circle `(726.23, 1307.82, r=230.85)`, upper green circle `(1054.19, 668.85, r=74.2)`, upper blue circle `(1299.0, 707.5, r=214.44)`, central orange circle `(1498.79, 1336.72, r=130.38)`, a right blue wedge bounding box `[1407,798,822,371]`, and the dominant red axis line from `[565,1024]` to `[1593,1338]`. `sixto_stage_graph.json` now transcribes the H1/N1..H4/N4 plus K1..K4 chain from the stage-strip crop as a structured graph with four detected stage circles, global image coordinates, partition types, and the source rules `K1 = Distance = Circumference/2; K2 = K1 x 0.62; K3 = K2 x 0.62; K4 = K3 x 0.62` and `H + N = 1 Revolution in time`. Honest verdict: this completes the requested first vectorization/transcription layer, but these are still image-frame geometry witnesses, not yet QA/RT law promotions.
